text: AI, or artificial intelligence, is already a part of our daily lives. But scientists are working on something more advanced: Artificial General Intelligence, or AGI. Unlike today's AI, which is designed for specific tasks, AGI would be able to learn and solve any problem a human can. This could bring huge benefits, such as curing diseases or solving climate change. However, risks are also real. Experts worry about job loss or even losing control over such powerful systems. Predictions about when AGI will arrive vary widely. Some think it could happen in the next decade, while others believe it is many decades away. The future of AI depends on how carefully we develop it.
